hero.subtitle: A team of specialized AI skills that share a persistent workspace — covering the full development lifecycle from requirements to tested code. Install once, ship end-to-end.
hero.statsTitle: A team of skills, each playing its role. Building the full development lifecycle.

problem.label: The Problem
problem.title: Every AI session starts from zero.
problem.intro: The project context you built yesterday — the architecture, the conventions, the domain knowledge — is gone today. Three things make this painful on real projects.

items.title: Context evaporates.
items.desc: Close the IDE, switch machines, come back next week — the AI has forgotten everything.

items.title: Same preamble every time.
items.desc: Re-explain the tech stack, re-state the conventions, re-describe the domain. Again and again.

items.title: No separation of concerns.
items.desc: Analysis, design, code, and tests jumbled in a single conversation. No structure, no handoffs.

problem.mentalModel: <accent>Mental model in 30 seconds:</accent> imagine hiring a tiny engineering team — Analyst, Architect, Developer, Reviewer, Tester — with a shared project notebook that lives in your repo. The workflow is standardized, so it works the same whether you're a senior or a junior. <accent>That's MVTT.</accent>

persistent.label: Persistent Context
persistent.title:
Context that grows
with your project.
persistent.desc: Context is never lost between conversations. Start a new AI Agent session tomorrow and it picks up exactly where you left off — your domain model, architecture decisions, in-progress tasks, and team conventions are all there.

features.title: Save progress
features.desc: Every skill automatically updates session.yaml with what was done.

features.title: Resume anywhere
features.desc: /mvt-resume restores full context in a new conversation.

features.title: Sync after changes
features.desc: /mvt-sync-context updates context when code evolves outside the workflow.

lines.comment: # Who did what, what's in progress

persistent.caption: Version-controlled. Team-shared. Travels with your repo.

workflow.label: The Workflow
workflow.title: One shared truth. Zero drift.
workflow.subtitle: When the Analyst discovers a domain concept, the Architect sees it. When the Architect makes a design decision, the Developer follows it.
states:
Your AI assistant is a generalist.
It can help — but it can't lead a workflow.
MVTT gives it a team. Every skill knows its role.

Design becomes code.
Code gets reviewed.
Reviewed gets tested.

Context flows through every phase.
Each step's output is the next step's input.
It accumulates. It doesn't reset.

Your team. One shared notebook.
Every session makes the next one better.

skills.label: The Skills
skills.title: Not commands. An integrated team.
skills.subtitle: Every skill's output becomes the next skill's input. Context compounds instead of evaporating.

items.useWhen: You have a new requirements doc or feature spec to break down.
items.desc: Extracts domain concepts, features, and acceptance criteria into structured analysis.

items.useWhen: Requirements are ready and you need architecture.
items.desc: Defines modules, boundaries, and data flow based on analyzed requirements.

items.useWhen: Design is too big for a single implement pass.
items.desc: Generates a tracked plan with ordered tasks and dependencies.

items.useWhen: Design and plan are ready to become code.
items.desc: Writes production code following the design and plan specifications.

items.useWhen: Implementation is written and needs quality check.
items.desc: Checks quality, standards compliance, and identifies potential issues.

items.useWhen: Implementation is reviewed and ready for tests.
items.desc: Generates tests that validate the implemented behavior.

items.useWhen: You suspect a bug and want diagnosis before any change.
items.desc: Investigates root cause and impact scope. Read-only — makes no code changes.

items.useWhen: Bug is confirmed and you're ready to resolve it.
items.desc: Diagnoses and fixes with full context awareness of the codebase.

items.useWhen: You want to restructure code without changing behavior.
items.desc: Refactors with awareness of architecture decisions and conventions.
Goes straight to implementation, skipping the full workflow ceremony.
Detect tech stack and initialize the workspace. Use on first time or major restructure.
Restore full context from a previous session. Continue exactly where you left off.
"Where am I in the workflow?" Shows progress and loaded context.
Reverse-analyze existing code into structured project context. Great for onboarding.
Reconcile workspace with code when changes happen outside the workflow.
Analyze token footprint and suggest optimizations when usage feels high.
Add, remove, or reorganize knowledge entries and registry bindings.
Archive stale artifacts and maintain workspace health.
Break epic-scale requirements into right-sized sub-changes with DAG dependencies.
Mark tasks done/blocked/skipped and auto-advance current_tasks in plan.yaml.
Change framework settings like language, output format, and preferences.
View and customize output templates for skill artifacts.
Scaffold a new custom skill interactively for your team's specific needs.
Show available skills, current status, and suggest what to do next.

recipes.label: Common Recipes
recipes.title: Pick the recipe that fits.
recipes.subtitle: You don't always need every skill. Each recipe is a starting point — /mvt-help suggests the next step based on your project's actual state.

whoIsFor.label: Who This Is For
whoIsFor.title:
For developers who use AI Agent
on real, ongoing projects.
whoIsFor.subtitle: Not one-off scripts. Real codebases with real context that needs to persist.
fit: You maintain a medium-sized codebase (5k+ LOC) and feel context-loss pain every day.
You want a structured workflow even when working solo — analysis, design, implement, review, test.
You lead a team where AI proficiency varies — MVTT standardizes the workflow so juniors and seniors produce consistent output.
You want a project notebook that travels with the repo — version-controlled, team-shared, bilingual (EN/CN).

skip.title: Skip MVTT if you...
items: Build throwaway scripts (regular AI Agent is faster)
Need enterprise CI/CD pipelines or role-based access control
Prefer a single CLAUDE.md and rolling your own prompts

items.q: How is MVTT different from a well-written CLAUDE.md?
items.a: A CLAUDE.md gives Claude instructions. MVTT gives Claude a team — specialized skills with shared memory, dedicated templates, and a handoff protocol. CLAUDE.md is a memo; MVTT is an org chart. You can use both; they don't conflict.

items.q: Does it explode my token usage?
items.a: No. The workspace is file-based and on-demand. MVTT loads only the context that's relevant to the current skill (default relevance threshold: 70%). Run /mvt-check-context to see your token footprint and optimize.
No. Set preferences.interaction_language: zh-CN and the framework switches to Chinese — both in chat and in generated artifacts. Bilingual projects are first-class.

items.q: Can my team use it together?
items.a: Yes. The .ai-agents/ workspace is version-controlled, so it travels with your repo. Everyone on the team sees the same context, the same plans, the same history. Just commit .ai-agents/ to your repo.

items.q: Why so many skills? Isn't that overkill?
items.a: Most teams use 5-8 skills regularly. The others are there for specific situations (epic decomposition, context sync, output templating) — you don't need to learn them up front. Run /mvt-help and it tells you exactly which one to use next.

install.label: Get Started
install.title:
One command to
install your team.
install.subtitle: Install MVTT into any project. A full team of specialized AI skills, ready in under a minute. No config needed. Works with any existing codebase.
